seoTitle: How to Add Custom HTML Code to Elementor in WordPress
metaDescription: Embed custom HTML, scripts, and widgets in Elementor using the built-in HTML widget. This guide covers safe code placement and styling with CSS.
intro: Elementor's HTML widget makes it possible to drop in custom code, embed external widgets, or add scripts without touching your theme's files directly. This guide covers adding the HTML widget to your page, pasting in your code, and applying CSS or JS styling so it fits the rest of your design. The method works with both the free and Pro versions of Elementor across WordPress themes.

steps.heading: Open the Elementor editor
steps.body: Launch the Elementor editor on the page where you want to add custom HTML.

steps.heading: Add the HTML widget
steps.body: Search for the HTML widget in Elementor's widget panel and drag it onto the section of your page where the code should appear.

steps.heading: Paste your custom HTML code
steps.body: Enter your HTML code, embed snippet, or script directly into the widget's code field.

steps.heading: Apply CSS or JS styling if needed
steps.body: Add any additional CSS or JavaScript required to style the embedded element so it matches the rest of your page.

steps.heading: Save and preview the page
steps.body: Save your changes and preview the page to confirm the custom HTML displays and functions correctly before publishing.

faq.question: Do I need Elementor Pro to add HTML code to my page?
faq.answer: No, the HTML widget is available in both Elementor Free and Elementor Pro, so you don't need the paid version just to embed custom code.

faq.question: Can adding custom HTML break my WordPress site?
faq.answer: Poorly formatted or conflicting code can cause display issues, so it's worth previewing changes before publishing and undoing anything that looks off.

faq.question: Can I add JavaScript through the Elementor HTML widget, not just HTML?
faq.answer: Yes, the HTML widget accepts script tags along with HTML, so you can embed JavaScript-driven widgets or custom functionality the same way.

faq.question: Will custom HTML added through Elementor work with any WordPress theme?
faq.answer: Yes, since the HTML widget renders independently of the theme's templates, it should work consistently across different WordPress themes.
